description: Named ffmpeg filter recipes for HELEN video assembly. Callers reference by key; bodies are plain filter strings with {placeholders} for runtime params (duration, fps, size).

effects.name: ken_burns_slow_in
effects.description: Slow zoom-in from 1.00 to 1.08, center hold

effects.name: ken_burns_slow_out
effects.description: Slow zoom-out from 1.10 to 1.00, center hold

effects.name: ken_burns_pan_left_to_center
effects.description: Zoom 1.10→1.00 while panning from left to center

effects.name: xfade_fade
effects.description: Hard fade to black between clips

effects.name: grade_warm_golden
effects.description: Warm golden-hour tint — saturation + brightness up

effects.name: grade_cool_twilight
effects.description: Cool twilight — saturation down, slight blue shift

effects.name: grade_manga_bw
effects.description: Desaturate for manga-style B&W; requires downstream dither for real manga look

placeholders.{frames}: integer, total frames for the effect (duration_sec * fps)
placeholders.{offset}: float seconds — for xfade (when the transition starts)

usage_contract.composition: Motion + color effects chain with commas. Transitions use fragment form, composed separately in filter_complex graph.
usage_contract.caller_responsibility: Caller substitutes placeholders before passing to ffmpeg. Registry does NOT evaluate or execute — it's a lookup table.

gaps: No per-channel color curves (only global eq) — add for finer grade control
No LUT application — could add for operator-custom 3D LUTs if desired
No text/drawtext recipes — handled separately by caption skills
No motion blur — would require minterpolate, expensive
No stabilization — our input is already stable stills
